prompt:
Our dashboard service pulls live status updates over a Server-Sent-Events stream. We have three implementations in different packages and none of them handles the full SSE line protocol: one drops multi-line data fields (they get joined incorrectly), one ignores the id field so reconnects always start from the beginning, and one tries to work with raw Buffers instead of text lines and misses events when a chunk cuts across a line boundary. I need ssefeed.ts — stdlib only, no npm, node 26 with type stripping (no enums, no namespaces, explicit .ts import extensions). test_ssefeed.ts is the acceptance contract: `node --test test_ssefeed.ts` fully green means done; test file read-only.

Transport: (lastEventId: string | null) => AsyncGenerator<string> — an async generator that yields text segments (plain strings). The consumer calls it when connecting or reconnecting, passing the last event id it received (null on the first call). The generator yields zero or more text segments and then returns (stream closed). The consumer then calls it again to reconnect.

export class SseEvent { type: string; data: string; id: string | null; constructor(type, data, id) }

export class SseFeed
  constructor(transport: (lastEventId: string | null) => AsyncGenerator<string>)
  retryMs: number  (default 3000; updated by retry: fields)

  events(): AsyncGenerator<SseEvent>
    Parses the SSE line protocol per the WHATWG EventSource spec:
    - Lines are delimited by \n (may arrive split across text segments).
    - A line starting with : is a comment and is ignored.
    - A non-empty line with a colon: field name = text before the first colon; value = text after (with exactly one leading space stripped if present).
    - A non-empty line with no colon: field name = the entire line, value = empty string.
    - An empty line (blank line) dispatches the current event buffer if the data buffer is non-empty; then resets the buffer.
    - event: field → sets the event type for the current event (default 'message').
    - data: field → appends to data buffer (multiple data fields join with \n between them).
    - id: field → sets the current event id (remembered for reconnect).
    - retry: field (numeric) → updates retryMs; never dispatches an event.
    - Dispatch: when a blank line fires and data is non-empty, yield an SseEvent with {type, data, id as it was when the blank line arrived}; then reset type/data for the next event.
    - When the transport generator closes, reconnect by calling the transport again with the last received event id. The generator never returns on its own — only a break from the caller terminates it.
    - id field carries across events within a session; also sent to the transport on reconnect.
